raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting a complaint against Barclays Bank for mishandling two disputes totaling {$10000.00} related to a failed refund from a merchant called Ascend Trading, whose services were processed through XXXX. 

I only signed up for Ascend Trading 's trading software because of their XXXX money-back guarantee. I used the software exactly as instructed, but I lost money using it and followed every step required to qualify for a refund. In XX/XX/year>, Ascend Trading even sent me a written Refund Agreement confirming I was eligible. But after signing it, they never issued the refund, stopped responding, and still haven't refunded me. 

I opened my first dispute with Barclays on XX/XX/year>, and to my shock, they denied it claiming I didnt use the software for the XXXX period. This is completely false. I provided screenshots and written confirmations from the merchant showing I met the refund terms. 

I reopened the dispute with even more documentation ( attached ), yet on XX/XX/year>, Barclays mailed me letters saying you confirmed you are no longer disputing this transaction which is absolutely false. I never made any such confirmation. 

I re-submitted my case again on XX/XX/year> and faxed in all documentation. Many days passed with no response whatsoever no letter, no email, and no phone call confirming receipt. So I called Barclays on XX/XX/XXXX. The representative XXXX XXXX XXXX could not answer any of my questions : she couldnt explain the status of my case, confirm if I was being charged interest, or even escalate the call to a supervisor. I was told to just wait. 

Finally, on XX/XX/XXXX, I called again and learned that the disputes had been closed again because the merchant says " they are not liable. This is completely unfair and inaccurate. 

Ive sent multiple emails to XXXX and received no reply whatsoever. 

I am now being charged interest on these {$10000.00} charges, and Barclays has made numerous factual errors, failed to communicate, and has refused to protect me as a consumer. 

Requested Resolution : Reopen the dispute Remove all interest charges Re-apply temporary credit while the case is investigated Investigate the false statement that I withdrew the dispute Consumer Protections Cited : Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( XXXX ), cardholders have the right to : Dispute charges for goods or services not received or not delivered as promised Withhold payment during a dispute investigation Barclays has violated both the timelines and communication requirements under XXXX and has failed to uphold its duty to protect consumers from merchant fraud despite receiving substantial documentation.
